raw.objective: “the applicability and performance of pharmacokinetic screening in detecting pharmacokinetic interactions between valproic acid and carbamazepine”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, AGE, DDVPA, SEX, Comedication (PHB, CBZ) Included: WT, DDVPA, SEX, PHB, CBZ
raw.observations: The paper describes a simplified PK model. A nonlinear relationship between CL and patient characteristic is found. WT is most important covariate, females show lower VPA CL than males, comedication results in an increase in relative CL. VPA CL is large in younger children but decreases as patients mature, a non-linear relation between and maturing, decreasing CL.
raw.dose: Steady-state, Multiple- oral doses in patients
raw.equations: CL = (theta1*(WT**theta2)*(DMGKG**theta3)*(theta4**(1-SEX))*(theta7**PHB)*(theta5**CBZ))*(1+eta1) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) DMGKG: VPA dose in mg/kg/day WT: Total body weight (kg) SEX: gender (1: female, 0: male) PHB : comedication phenobarbital (1 :yes, 0:no) CBZ: comedication carbamazepine (1: yes, 0:no)
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 15.6/1000 theta2= 0.748 theta3= 0.183 theta4= 0.898 theta5= 0.769 theta6= 0.179 theta7= 1.1 omega1= 0.021 sigma1=0.034

extraction_target.title: Population-based investigation of valproic acid relative clearance using nonlinear mixed effects modeling: influence of drug-drug interaction and patient characteristics.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL = (theta1*(WT**theta2)*(DMGKG**theta3)*(theta4**(1-SEX))*(theta7**PHB)*(theta5**CBZ))*(1+eta1) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) DMGKG: VPA dose in mg/kg/day WT: Total body weight (kg) SEX: gender (1: female, 0: male) PHB : comedication phenobarbital (1 :yes, 0:no) CBZ: comedication carbamazepine (1: yes, 0:no)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady-state, Multiple- oral doses in patients
study.objective: “the applicability and performance of pharmacokinetic screening in detecting pharmacokinetic interactions between valproic acid and carbamazepine”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, AGE, DDVPA, SEX, Comedication (PHB, CBZ) Included: WT, DDVPA, SEX, PHB, CBZ

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
